summary: New password-reset flow mints the reset token via Math.random().toString(36).slice(2). Non-CSPRNG output (xorshift128+) is state-recoverable from a handful of observations, enabling silent account takeover for any victim the attacker targets. Storage path is fine; the generation call is the bug.

must_not_find.reason: Storing the reset token via db.user.update is the conventional pattern for short-lived bearer secrets; the bug is in generation, not persistence. Reviewer must not invent a 'tokens stored in plaintext' BLOCK finding on the update call.

Severity must not be downgraded to FIX-FOLLOWUP or NIT. Predictable password-reset tokens are account-takeover-grade and must block merge. A finding worded as 'consider using crypto.randomBytes' at non-BLOCK severity fails the calibration test.

tolerance.note: Reviewer may legitimately surface a FIX-FOLLOWUP for the missing token TTL (no resetTokenExpiresAt column written, no single-use/consume semantics). One such adjacent finding is acceptable and does not affect grading.
